g.text: The RISC-V vs ARM debate for edge AI comes down to a fundamental tradeoff between
purpose-built for neural network inference, while ARM provides a battle-tested
The leading RISC-V implementations for edge AI include SiFive's X280 with vector
extensions, Kendryte's K210 dual-core processor, and ESP32-C3 for ultra-low-power
applications. The key advantage is the ability to add custom instructions for
specific neural network operations without licensing fees [1][3].
ARM's Cortex-M55 paired with the Ethos-U55 NPU represents the current state of the
art for embedded ML inference. The CMSIS-NN library provides optimized kernels, and
the toolchain maturity means faster time-to-market [2][4]...
